config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap jadi batas utama; kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu didahulukan tanpa batas, itu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan atas nama “kebaikan bersama”. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat memang penting, tetapi kepentingan umum hanya boleh dibatasi sejauh benar-benar perlu dan tetap menghormati martabat serta kebebasan tiap orang. Kalau hak individu diabaikan, yang rugi bukan cuma satu orang, melainkan juga keadilan sosial dalam jangka panjang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society matter, but they should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From an American perspective, I am wary of any idea that lets “the common good” become a blank check for the government or the majority to override basic rights, because that has too often been used to justify abuse. At the same time, individual rights are not a license to ignore public safety or the harm your choices can cause other people, so there are real limits. For me, the right balance is that society can restrict individual freedom only when it is clearly necessary, narrowly tailored, and accountable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu, karena di Indonesia pun alasan “demi kebaikan bersama” sering dipakai untuk menekan suara kecil dan membungkam kritik. Saya melihat kepentingan umum memang penting untuk hal-hal seperti kesehatan publik, ketertiban, dan keselamatan, tetapi pembatasannya harus jelas, proporsional, dan tidak boleh merendahkan martabat orang. Kalau hak individu terlalu mudah dikorbankan, yang muncul bukan solidaritas, melainkan kekuasaan yang kebablasan. Jadi, prioritas masyarakat hanya sah sejauh benar-benar perlu dan tetap berada di bawah pagar hak dasar.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From my U.S. perspective, I am skeptical of any broad claim that the common good justifies overriding basic rights, because in American history that language has often been used to excuse discrimination, censorship, and government overreach. At the same time, I do not think individual rights are absolute in every case, because public health, safety, and preventing harm to others can justify limited restrictions. The key is that those limits must be narrow, transparent, and accountable, not a blanket excuse to subordinate the person to the crowd.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu sebagai aturan umum. Sebagai orang Indonesia, saya melihat betapa sering alasan “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai untuk membungkam kritik, memperkecil suara minoritas, atau melegitimasi kebijakan yang tidak akuntabel. Memang ada situasi tertentu seperti kesehatan publik dan keselamatan yang bisa membatasi kebebasan, tetapi pembatasan itu harus ketat, proporsional, dan tidak boleh menghapus martabat orang. Kalau tidak, yang disebut kepentingan masyarakat justru berubah menjadi alat kekuasaan yang mudah disalahgunakan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support making society's interests generally outrank individual rights, because in the United States that idea has too often been used to excuse abuse in the name of order, security, or the majority. I do think there are real cases where public health and safety justify limited restrictions, but those limits have to be narrow, transparent, and subject to real oversight. If we let "the common good" become a vague trump card, it becomes too easy to silence minorities and chip away at basic freedoms. To me, a healthy society protects people precisely by treating individual rights as the starting point, not the obstacle.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
